name: Old Country Buttermilk Pretzels
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup buttermilk or rehydrated buttermilk powder
recipeInstructions:
This is truly a gourmet pretzel -- a step beyond traditional recipes from the Old Country. Excellent with beer or cider, but absolutely irresistible spread with cold sour cream.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
In a small bowl, combine yeast with sugar and warm water. Set aside in a warm place until foam forms, about 5-10 minutes.
In a saucepan, add butter to buttermilk and bring to boil. Stir to melt completely if necessary.
Transfer mixture to a bowl, add cold water, stir, and allow it to cool to lukewarm.
In a large bowl or food processor, combine flour and salt. Stir in yeast mixture and cooled buttermilk mixture until well mixed.
Knead dough by hand on a lightly floured surface or pulse about 15-20 times in food processor until smooth and springy.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl and turn to coat all sides. Cover with damp towel and set at warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
Punch down dough and knead a few strokes. Wrap tightly in plastic wrap or wax paper and refrigerate until well chilled, about 1 hour.
Using a knife, cut dough into 25 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a cylinder, 10-12 inches long (or 14-16 inches for larger pretzels).
Shape each cylinder by forming a loop, twisting ends, and attaching them to the bottom of the loop.
Bring 2 quarts water to boil. Add baking soda and remaining sugar to water.
Drop pretzels into water one at a time using a slotted spoon; remove as they float to surface in about 2-3 seconds.
Place pretzels on towel right side up to drain for a few minutes, then onto a baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Brush tops with beaten egg and sprinkle lightly with coarse salt.
Bake for 15-25 minutes until well browned. Cool on rack. Yield: 15 large or 25 small pretzels.
